notes: This dataset is for informational UI/demo use and not a substitute for medical advice.
Conventional medicine entries are phrased as 'commonly used for' rather than 'cures'.
Herbal/Ayurvedic entries are phrased as 'traditional use' or 'wellness support' where appropriate.
The image field is intentionally blank so you can attach your own verified images in the frontend.

source_collections.label: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements Fact Sheets
Botanical Survey of India Medicinal Plant Database

items.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
items.summary: Commonly used to reduce fever and relieve mild to moderate pain.
items.caution: Do not exceed the recommended dose because high doses can harm the liver.

items.name: Ibuprofen
items.summary: Commonly used for pain, swelling, inflammation, and fever.
items.caution: May irritate the stomach and is not suitable for everyone with ulcers, kidney disease, or some heart conditions.

items.name: Naproxen
items.summary: Commonly used for pain, inflammation, arthritis symptoms, and menstrual cramps.
items.caution: May increase stomach bleeding risk and should be used carefully in kidney disease.

items.name: Diclofenac
items.summary: Commonly used for musculoskeletal pain, joint pain, and inflammation.
items.caution: Can affect the stomach, heart, or kidneys in some people.

items.name: Cetirizine
items.summary: Commonly used for allergy symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itching.
items.caution: Can cause drowsiness in some people.

items.name: Loratadine
items.summary: Commonly used to relieve allergy symptoms such as runny nose, sneezing, and watery eyes.
items.caution: Use the recommended dose and check before combining with other cold-allergy products.

items.name: Fexofenadine
items.summary: Commonly used for seasonal allergies and hives with itching.
items.caution: Avoid taking with certain fruit juices because they may affect absorption.

items.name: Levocetirizine
items.summary: Commonly used to relieve allergy symptoms and hives.
items.caution: May cause sleepiness in some users.

items.name: Chlorpheniramine
items.summary: Commonly used for cold and allergy symptoms such as runny nose and sneezing.
items.caution: Often causes drowsiness and dry mouth.

items.name: Diphenhydramine
items.summary: Commonly used for allergy symptoms, itching, and some cold symptoms.
items.caution: Can strongly cause drowsiness; use carefully.

items.name: Omeprazole
items.summary: Commonly used for acid reflux, heartburn, and GERD.
items.caution: Long-term use should be medically reviewed.

items.name: Pantoprazole
items.summary: Commonly used to reduce stomach acid in GERD and related conditions.
items.caution: Long-term use should be checked by a clinician.

items.name: Rabeprazole
items.summary: Commonly used for acid reflux, GERD, and stomach-acid-related symptoms.
items.caution: Long-term use should be reviewed by a healthcare professional.

items.name: Famotidine
items.summary: Commonly used for heartburn, acid indigestion, and some reflux symptoms.
items.caution: Dose adjustment may be needed in kidney problems.

items.name: Calcium Carbonate Antacid
items.summary: Commonly used for quick relief of heartburn and acid indigestion.
items.caution: Do not overuse because excessive intake may cause constipation or other issues.

items.name: Ondansetron
items.summary: Commonly used to prevent or treat nausea and vomiting.
items.caution: May affect heart rhythm in some people; follow prescribed directions.

items.name: Domperidone
items.summary: Commonly used for nausea, vomiting, and some motility-related stomach symptoms.
items.caution: Not appropriate for everyone and may affect heart rhythm in some patients.

items.name: Metoclopramide
items.summary: Commonly used for nausea, vomiting, and delayed stomach emptying in selected cases.
items.caution: Should not be used longer than advised because it can cause movement-related side effects.

items.name: Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS)
items.summary: Commonly used to replace fluids and electrolytes lost from diarrhea or vomiting.
items.caution: Severe dehydration requires medical care.

items.name: Loperamide
items.summary: Commonly used for short-term relief of diarrhea.
items.caution: Do not self-use for bloody diarrhea, high fever, or severe infection concerns without medical advice.

items.name: Bismuth Subsalicylate
items.summary: Commonly used for upset stomach, nausea, and diarrhea.
items.caution: Contains a salicylate; not suitable for everyone, especially some children or people with aspirin-related issues.

items.name: Dicyclomine
items.summary: Commonly used to reduce intestinal muscle spasm and cramping.
items.caution: May cause dry mouth, dizziness, or blurred vision.

items.name: Lactulose
items.summary: Commonly used for constipation and sometimes for other clinician-directed uses.
items.caution: May cause bloating or gas.

items.name: Polyethylene Glycol 3350
items.summary: Commonly used to soften stool and relieve constipation.
items.caution: Use as directed and seek advice if constipation is persistent.

items.name: Senna
items.summary: Commonly used for short-term relief of constipation.
items.caution: Long-term overuse can cause dependence on laxatives.

items.name: Psyllium Husk (Isabgol)
items.summary: Commonly used to improve bowel regularity and help with constipation.
items.caution: Take with enough water to avoid choking or blockage.

items.name: Amoxicillin
items.summary: Commonly used for certain bacterial infections such as ear, throat, or respiratory infections.
items.caution: Does not treat viral infections such as the common cold.

items.name: Amoxicillin + Clavulanate
items.summary: Commonly used for certain bacterial infections, including some sinus, skin, and respiratory infections.
items.caution: Use only when prescribed and complete the course as advised.

items.name: Azithromycin
items.summary: Commonly used for selected bacterial infections such as some throat, ear, lung, and skin infections.
items.caution: Does not work for viral infections and may affect heart rhythm in some people.

items.name: Clarithromycin
items.summary: Commonly used for certain bacterial infections affecting the lungs, skin, or throat.
items.caution: May interact with many medicines.

items.name: Doxycycline
items.summary: Commonly used for certain bacterial infections and also for acne in some cases.
items.caution: Can increase sun sensitivity and is not suitable in all age groups or during some pregnancies.

items.name: Ciprofloxacin
items.summary: Commonly used for selected bacterial infections, including some urinary tract infections.
items.caution: Has important safety warnings and should only be used when appropriate.

items.name: Cephalexin
items.summary: Commonly used for certain bacterial infections such as skin or throat infections.
items.caution: Does not treat viral illnesses.

items.name: Cefixime
items.summary: Commonly used for selected bacterial infections including some urinary or respiratory infections.
items.caution: Take only on medical advice and complete the prescribed course.

items.name: Metronidazole
items.summary: Commonly used for certain anaerobic bacterial infections and some protozoal infections.
items.caution: Alcohol should be avoided around treatment as advised by the clinician/pharmacist.

items.name: Nitrofurantoin
items.summary: Commonly used for certain urinary tract infections.
items.caution: Not suitable for all kidney function levels.

items.name: Fluconazole
items.summary: Commonly used for selected fungal infections such as some yeast infections.
items.caution: May interact with other medicines and affect the liver in some cases.

items.name: Clotrimazole
items.summary: Commonly used for topical treatment of fungal skin infections and some yeast infections.
items.caution: Use the correct formulation for the affected area.

items.name: Miconazole
items.summary: Commonly used for fungal infections such as athlete's foot, jock itch, or ringworm.
items.caution: Use as directed for the full treatment period.

items.name: Acyclovir
items.summary: Commonly used for herpes virus infections including cold sores, genital herpes, or shingles in selected forms.
items.caution: Works best when started early in the course of symptoms.

items.name: Oseltamivir
items.summary: Commonly used for treatment or prevention of influenza in selected cases.
items.caution: Most effective when started early after flu symptoms begin.

items.name: Salbutamol (Albuterol)
items.summary: Commonly used for quick relief of wheezing and breathing difficulty in asthma or similar conditions.
items.caution: Frequent need for reliever inhaler may mean poor control and needs medical review.

items.name: Budesonide Inhalation
items.summary: Commonly used for regular control of asthma inflammation.
items.caution: It is a controller medicine, not a quick-relief rescue inhaler.

items.name: Fluticasone Nasal Spray
items.summary: Commonly used for allergy-related sneezing, stuffy nose, and runny nose.
items.caution: Works best with regular use as directed.

items.name: Montelukast
items.summary: Commonly used for asthma prevention and some allergy symptoms.
items.caution: It is not for immediate asthma attacks and has important mental health warnings.

items.name: Metformin
items.summary: Commonly used to help control blood sugar in type 2 diabetes.
items.caution: May cause stomach upset and is not suitable for some kidney conditions.

items.name: Glimepiride
items.summary: Commonly used to lower blood sugar in type 2 diabetes.
items.caution: Can cause low blood sugar in some people.

items.name: Insulin Glargine
items.summary: Commonly used as a long-acting insulin to help control blood sugar.
items.caution: Insulin dosing must be individualized; low blood sugar can occur.

items.name: Amlodipine
items.summary: Commonly used for high blood pressure and some types of chest pain.
items.caution: May cause swelling of the feet or ankles in some users.

items.name: Losartan
diseases: Kidney protection in selected diabetes cases
items.summary: Commonly used for high blood pressure and kidney protection in some people with diabetes.
items.caution: Avoid in pregnancy unless specifically directed by a physician.

items.name: Telmisartan
items.summary: Commonly used to treat high blood pressure.
items.caution: Needs medical supervision, especially in pregnancy or kidney issues.

items.name: Enalapril
items.summary: Commonly used for high blood pressure and some forms of heart failure.
items.caution: Can cause cough and is not used in pregnancy.

items.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
items.summary: Commonly used for high blood pressure and fluid retention.
items.caution: Can affect sodium, potassium, and other electrolytes.

items.name: Atenolol
items.summary: Commonly used for high blood pressure and some heart-related conditions including angina.
items.caution: Should not usually be stopped suddenly without advice.

items.name: Metoprolol
items.summary: Commonly used for high blood pressure and certain heart conditions.
items.caution: May cause tiredness or dizziness; do not stop abruptly.

items.name: Atorvastatin
items.summary: Commonly used to lower cholesterol and reduce cardiovascular risk.
items.caution: Report unexplained muscle pain and discuss liver monitoring if advised.

items.name: Rosuvastatin
items.summary: Commonly used to lower cholesterol and reduce risk of heart-related events in selected patients.
items.caution: Can cause muscle-related side effects in some users.

items.name: Aspirin Low Dose
diseases: Antiplatelet use in selected cardiovascular conditions
items.summary: Commonly used in selected patients to reduce clot-related cardiovascular risk.
items.caution: Not for everyone; bleeding risk must be considered.

items.name: Clopidogrel
diseases: Antiplatelet use after selected heart or stroke conditions
items.summary: Commonly used to help prevent harmful blood clots in selected heart or stroke-related conditions.
items.caution: Can increase bleeding risk.

items.name: Levothyroxine
items.summary: Commonly used to treat hypothyroidism by replacing thyroid hormone.
items.caution: Best taken consistently as advised and not used for weight loss.

items.name: Prednisone
items.summary: Commonly used to reduce inflammation and suppress immune reactions in selected conditions.
items.caution: Should not be stopped suddenly after longer use unless medically guided.

items.name: Hydrocortisone Cream
items.summary: Commonly used for short-term relief of mild itchy or inflamed skin conditions.
items.caution: Avoid prolonged unsupervised use, especially on the face or broken skin.

items.name: Triamcinolone Cream
items.summary: Commonly used for inflammatory skin conditions such as eczema or dermatitis.
items.caution: Use only as directed and avoid overuse.

items.name: Mupirocin
items.summary: Commonly used for certain bacterial skin infections such as impetigo.
items.caution: Not for fungal or viral skin infections.

items.name: Permethrin
items.summary: Commonly used to treat scabies or head lice depending on the product.
items.caution: Use the correct product and repeat schedule exactly as directed.

items.name: Calamine Lotion
items.summary: Commonly used for minor itching, insect bites, and mild skin irritation.
items.caution: For external use only.

items.name: Ferrous Sulfate
items.summary: Commonly used to treat or prevent iron deficiency.
items.caution: Can cause stomach upset or constipation and should be kept out of reach of children.

items.name: Calcium + Vitamin D
items.summary: Commonly used to support bone health when calcium or vitamin D intake is inadequate.
items.caution: Excess supplementation may not be appropriate for everyone.

items.name: Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol)
items.summary: Commonly used to prevent or treat vitamin D deficiency and support bone health.
items.caution: Do not take high doses without advice.

items.name: Vitamin B12 (Cyanocobalamin)
items.summary: Commonly used to treat or prevent vitamin B12 deficiency.
items.caution: Deficiency should ideally be medically evaluated to find the cause.

items.name: Folic Acid
items.summary: Commonly used to prevent or treat folate deficiency and in pregnancy supplementation plans.
items.caution: Persistent anemia needs proper evaluation rather than self-treatment alone.

items.name: Zinc Sulfate
items.summary: Commonly used to treat or prevent zinc deficiency.
items.caution: Excess zinc can interfere with copper balance and cause stomach upset.

items.name: Magnesium Hydroxide
items.summary: Commonly used as a laxative and in some products as an antacid.
items.caution: Use carefully in kidney disease.

items.name: Tamsulosin
items.summary: Commonly used to improve urinary symptoms from enlarged prostate.
items.caution: May cause dizziness, especially when standing up.

items.name: Sildenafil
items.summary: Commonly used for erectile dysfunction and, in other forms, certain pulmonary hypertension indications.
items.caution: Must not be combined with nitrate medicines.

items.name: Finasteride
items.summary: Commonly used for enlarged prostate and, in another strength, male pattern hair loss.
items.caution: Pregnant women should avoid handling crushed tablets.

items.name: Allopurinol
items.summary: Commonly used to lower uric acid and help prevent gout attacks over time.
items.caution: Serious rash can occur in rare cases and needs urgent evaluation.

items.name: Colchicine
diseases: Gout flare prevention and treatment in selected use
items.summary: Commonly used in selected gout treatment or prevention plans.
items.caution: Dose matters greatly and many drug interactions exist.

items.name: Melatonin
items.summary: Commonly used for some sleep timing issues or jet lag.
items.caution: Sleep problems can have many causes and persistent insomnia needs evaluation.

items.name: Nicotine Replacement Gum / Patch
items.summary: Commonly used to support quitting smoking by reducing withdrawal symptoms.
items.caution: Use the correct strength and product instructions.

items.name: Ashwagandha
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda and commonly marketed for stress and sleep support.
items.caution: Herbal products can interact with medicines; avoid assuming they are risk-free.

items.name: Turmeric / Curcumin
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda and commonly used in supplements for inflammation-related support.
items.caution: May interact with blood-thinning medicines in some cases.

items.name: Ginger
items.summary: Commonly used for nausea support and digestive discomfort.
items.caution: Use cautiously with blood-thinning medicines or before surgery.

items.name: Peppermint Oil
items.summary: Commonly used for digestive symptom support, including some irritable bowel symptoms.
items.caution: Can worsen reflux in some people.

items.name: Aloe Vera Gel
items.summary: Commonly used topically for minor skin irritation and soothing support.
items.caution: Do not assume all oral aloe products are safe or suitable.

items.name: Chamomile
items.summary: Commonly used as an herbal tea or supplement for relaxation and mild digestive comfort support.
items.caution: People with some plant allergies should use caution.

items.name: Tulsi (Holy Basil)
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda for respiratory comfort and general wellness support.
items.caution: Evidence and product quality vary; discuss herb-drug interactions when relevant.

items.name: Amla (Indian Gooseberry)
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda for digestive wellness and general health support.
items.caution: Product quality and concentration vary between preparations.

items.name: Brahmi (Bacopa monnieri)
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda and commonly marketed for memory and cognitive support.
items.caution: May cause stomach upset in some users.

items.name: Fenugreek (Methi)
items.summary: Traditionally used for digestive support and sometimes for blood-sugar-related wellness support.
items.caution: People on diabetes medicines should be careful because additive effects are possible.

items.name: Garlic
items.summary: Commonly used in food and supplements for general cardiovascular wellness support.
items.caution: Can increase bleeding risk in some situations or with some medicines.

items.name: Cinnamon
items.summary: Commonly used as a spice and supplement for traditional digestive and metabolic support.
items.caution: High supplemental doses may not be safe for everyone.

items.name: Licorice (Mulethi / Yashtimadhu)
items.summary: Traditionally used for throat comfort and digestive support.
items.caution: Large or prolonged intake can raise blood pressure in some forms.

items.name: Guggul
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda for joint and metabolic support.
items.caution: Product quality varies and medicine interactions are possible.

items.name: Triphala
items.summary: Traditional Ayurvedic formulation commonly used for digestive wellness and bowel regularity support.
items.caution: Use standardized products and avoid assuming all formulations are identical.

items.name: Neem
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda for skin and oral-care support.
items.caution: Not all neem preparations are suitable for internal use.

items.name: Shatavari
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda for women's wellness and digestive support.
items.caution: Evidence quality varies and herb-drug interactions remain possible.

items.name: Arjuna
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda for heart-related wellness support.
items.caution: Should not replace prescribed cardiovascular treatment.

items.name: Guduchi (Giloy)
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda for fever-related and general wellness support.
items.caution: Herbal liver-safety concerns have been reported with some products, so product quality and supervision matter.

items.name: Haritaki
items.summary: Traditionally used in Ayurveda for digestive health and bowel support.
items.caution: Use appropriate formulations and seek advice for persistent symptoms.
